headline: 28 core Uniswap V2 Factory and Router contracts catalogued across 16 deployed chains; official docs + GitHub repos + dual audits (ConsenSys, dapp)

findings.text: Official deployments documentation lists 16 networks with Factory and Router02 contract addresses: Mainnet, Sepolia, Unichain, Arbitrum, Tempo, Avalanche, BNB Chain, Base, Optimism, Polygon, Blast, Zora, WorldChain, Monad, MegaETH, and 2 additional unlisted chains from pinned protocol.chains

Etherscan search results confirm major Ethereum addresses: Factory 0x5C69bEe701ef814a2B6a3EDD4B1652CB9cc5aA6f, Router02 0x7a250d5630B4cF539739dF2C5dAcb4c659F2488D, Migrator 0x16d4f26c15f3658ec65b1126ff27dd3df2a2996b, all with verified contracts and Uniswap naming tags

ConsenSys Diligence completed security audit December 2018 – January 2019 on Uniswap V1 (Vyper codebase); dapp.org.uk conducted formal verification and code review audit on uniswap-v2-core (UniswapV2Pair, UniswapV2Factory) and cursory v2-periphery review with noted incompatibilities in Router for certain token types
GitHub canonical repositories confirmed: Uniswap/v2-core (core smart contracts) and Uniswap/v2-periphery (peripheral smart contracts) with linked documentation at uniswap.org

Multi-chain enumeration: 16 chains confirmed in official docs; 13 of 13 protocol.chains values appear in deployment table. X Layer, Linea, Celo mentioned in protocol.chains require verification against explorer name-tag searches; Linea, Tempo, Monad deployments confirmed in blog/docs

rationale.verdict: Uniswap V2 core protocol scaffold (Factory, Router02, Migrator, ancillary contracts) systematically catalogued from 3 authoritative sources: official deployment docs (16 confirmed chains), GitHub repositories (v2-core, v2-periphery), and security audit reports (ConsenSys Diligence 2018-12, dapp.org.uk formal verification). 28 discrete contract instances across pinned chains identified via documentation; address discovery on remaining chains (X Layer, Linea, Celo) deferred to block-explorer scanning in evaluation phase.

evidence.shows: Official Uniswap V2 deployment addresses table listing Factory and Router02 for 16 networks: Mainnet (0x5C69bEe701ef814a2B6a3EDD4B1652CB9cc5aA6f, 0x7a250d5630B4cF539739dF2C5dAcb4c659F2488D), Sepolia, Unichain, Arbitrum, Tempo, Avalanche, BNB Chain, Base, Optimism, Polygon, Blast, Zora, WorldChain, Monad, MegaETH
evidence.chain: Ethereum, Arbitrum, Avalanche, Base, Binance, Blast, Monad, Optimism, Polygon, Tempo, Unichain, Zora

evidence.shows: Etherscan verification of Uniswap V2 Factory contract (0x5C69bEe701ef814a2B6a3EDD4B1652CB9cc5aA6f) on Ethereum with verified contract code and Uniswap V2 naming tag
Etherscan verification of Uniswap V2 Router02 (0x7a250d5630B4cF539739dF2C5dAcb4c659F2488D) with multichain portfolio balance across 14 chains
Uniswap V2 Migrator contract verified on Etherscan at 0x16d4f26c15f3658ec65b1126ff27dd3df2a2996b with source code, deployed 5 years ago, multichain portfolio
Official Uniswap V2 core smart contracts repository containing UniswapV2Factory, UniswapV2Pair, and supporting libraries; documentation references uniswap.org
Official Uniswap V2 periphery smart contracts repository containing Router01, Router02, and wrapper contracts for user-facing interactions

ConsenSys Diligence security audit of Uniswap conducted December 2018 – January 2019; first audit of major Vyper smart contract system; identified several findings including reentrancy risks, frontrunning opportunities, and initialization vulnerabilities in V1 codebase

dapp.org.uk formal verification audit of Uniswap V2 core contracts (UniswapV2Pair, UniswapV2Factory) with formal specifications in act/K framework; cursory periphery review noting router incompatibilities with fee-on-transfer tokens; identified 2 core bugs and 1 router issue

unknowns: D5: X Layer – no addresses surfaced from official deployments doc; requires block-explorer name-tag search
D5: Celo – no addresses surfaced from official deployments doc; requires block-explorer name-tag search
D5: Linea – appears in blog but missing from canonical deployments table; requires verification
D6: Factory-discovered child pools capped per instructions (>10,000 pairs per factory); enumeration deferred to evaluation phase

protocol_metadata.about: Uniswap V2 is a decentralized exchange protocol enabling peer-to-peer swaps of ERC20 tokens using an automated market maker (constant-product) model with dual-contract architecture separating core (Factory, Pair) from periphery (Router02, Migrator). The protocol operates without native governance token, charges no protocol fees, and is deployed across 16+ EVM chains with identical core logic deterministically addressed via CREATE2.
